title: 7 Best Buttondown Alternatives in 2025 (Free & Paid)
meta_description: Find the best Buttondown alternatives for newsletters, email marketing & creators. Compare free & paid options with features & pricing.
h1: Best Buttondown Alternatives for Email Marketing & Newsletters
intro: While Buttondown excels for developers and technical writers who prefer markdown, it may not be the best fit for everyone. Whether you need more advanced automation, e-commerce integrations, or better monetization options, there are several powerful alternatives worth considering. We've compiled the top seven options to help you find the perfect email marketing platform for your specific needs.
why_switch: Need more advanced automation and segmentation features beyond Buttondown's core offering
Require integrations with e-commerce platforms, CRM systems, or other business tools
Looking for monetization options, ad networks, or built-in commerce features for your newsletter

alternatives.name: MailerLite
alternatives.tagline: Simple email marketing for small businesses
alternatives.best_for: small businesses and freelancers on a budget
alternatives.key_difference: MailerLite matches Buttondown's affordable $9 starting price while offering drag-and-drop editor, landing pages, and pop-ups—ideal if you prefer visual design over markdown. With 1,000 free subscribers and excellent customer support, it's perfect for non-technical users looking for Buttondown's affordability without the markdown requirement.

alternatives.name: Beehiiv
alternatives.tagline: The newsletter platform built for growth
alternatives.key_difference: Beehiiv is purpose-built for newsletter publishers with built-in monetization, an ad network, and referral programs—features Buttondown lacks. If you want to turn your newsletter into revenue, Beehiiv's growth-focused tools and generous 2,500 free subscriber limit make it the superior choice.

alternatives.name: ConvertKit
alternatives.key_difference: ConvertKit offers visual automation builders, landing pages, and built-in commerce features designed specifically for creators and course sellers. If you need to sell digital products or manage complex subscriber journeys beyond email, ConvertKit's creator-focused ecosystem outpaces Buttondown's technical simplicity.

alternatives.name: ActiveCampaign
alternatives.best_for: businesses needing advanced automation and CRM
alternatives.key_difference: ActiveCampaign combines email marketing with full CRM, lead scoring, and site tracking—offering enterprise-grade automation that Buttondown's simpler platform cannot match. If you need sophisticated marketing automation and customer relationship management integrated into one system, ActiveCampaign is the premium alternative.

alternatives.name: Mailchimp
alternatives.key_difference: Mailchimp provides a free tier for up to 500 subscribers with robust features including A/B testing, audience segmentation, and landing pages. While it has lower user satisfaction ratings, its established ecosystem and free tier make it a solid choice for beginners wanting more marketing tools than Buttondown offers.

alternatives.name: Brevo
alternatives.key_difference: Brevo uniquely offers an enormous free tier with up to 100,000 subscribers, plus built-in SMS, WhatsApp, and live chat capabilities alongside email. If you need multi-channel communication and transactional email APIs at no cost, Brevo's unlimited free subscriber limit demolishes Buttondown's 100-subscriber restriction.

alternatives.name: GetResponse
alternatives.best_for: online course creators and webinar hosts
alternatives.key_difference: GetResponse integrates native webinar hosting, online course creation, and conversion funnel builders—features completely absent from Buttondown. If you're a course creator or run webinars, GetResponse's all-in-one platform eliminates the need for separate tools.

faq.q: What is the best free alternative to Buttondown?
faq.a: Brevo offers the most generous free tier with up to 100,000 subscribers at no cost, making it ideal if you don't want to pay anything upfront. MailerLite and Beehiiv also provide robust free plans (1,000 and 2,500 subscribers respectively) with better features than Buttondown's 100-subscriber limit. However, if you specifically need markdown support and API access like Buttondown, you'll need to pay for most alternatives.

faq.q: What is the best Buttondown alternative for small business?
faq.a: MailerLite is the best alternative for small businesses, offering the same $9 starting price as Buttondown while providing drag-and-drop editing, landing pages, pop-ups, and excellent customer support. If you have a slightly larger budget, ConvertKit works well for service-based businesses selling digital products, while ActiveCampaign is ideal if you need CRM integration and advanced automation.

faq.q: Is there a cheaper alternative to Buttondown?
faq.a: Yes—Brevo is completely free for up to 100,000 subscribers, making it far more affordable than Buttondown's paid plans. MailerLite matches Buttondown's $9 starting price with more features included. If you want markdown support and paid subscription features at lower costs, Buttondown is actually quite competitive, but Brevo's free tier cannot be beaten for price.
